name: Howard County Police Department Call For Service - 2014-2023
description: Calls for Service by computer aided dispatch (CAD) event type, date, time, location, statistical reporting area (SRA), and beat.

name: Fire And Rescue Apparatus Responses: 2012 - 2018
provider: Department of Fire and Rescue Services

columns.name: Canceled_Enroute
columns.description: Was the unit's response canceled by 911 or Incident Command prior to arrival?

columns.name: Arrival_Time
columns.description: The time the unit arrived at the location of the incident

columns.name: Dispatch_Time
columns.description: The time that the unit was alerted by the 911 Center

columns.name: Enroute_Scene_Time
columns.description: The time the unit began travel to the scene

columns.name: Responded_From_Quarters
columns.description: Was this unit located in its assigned station when alerted? (Often, a unit is dispatched while still returning from a previous call.)

columns.name: First_Arriving_Unit
columns.description: Was this unit reported as first unit to arrive on scene?

description: One record for each emergency response by a Fire Department unit, including dispatch and response times, action taken, and counts of personnel which responded. This is an expansion of data available in the Howard County Fire And Rescue Incidents data set. Further detail is available in the Howard County Fire and Rescue Personnel Responses data set.

name: Fire & Rescue Incidents: 2012 - Present
description: One record for each emergency on which the Fire Department sent apparatus and personnel. More detail is available in the Apparatus Response and Personnel Response data sets.

name: Fire & Rescue Personnel Responses: 2012 - 2018
provider: Department of Fire and Rescue Services
description: One record for each emergency response by a Fire Department member, including rank and career / volunteer / contingent status of personnel which responded. This is an expansion of data available in the Howard County Fire And Rescue Incidents data set. Further detail is available in the Howard County Fire and Rescue Personnel Responses data set.

name: Fire Dispatches: 2012 - 2018

columns.name: Incident_Date
columns.description: The date the incident was created.

columns.name: Sub_Type_Code
columns.description: The sub-type code. Note that 'ALS' is an advanced life support call, meaning a paramedic's services are required. BLS is a basic life support call, for which an EMT is required.

columns.name: ID
columns.description: A unique incident number for each call.

description: Emergency incidents created by HCPD's 911 Dispatch Center and classified as Fire, Rescue or Emergency Medical Service (EMS) calls. Not all of these incidents are created as cases, and thus not all of these incidents have a matching record in the Howard County Fire & Rescue Incidents table.

name: Vendors Receiving Payments of $30,000 or More: FY 2010 - FY 2024
description: This dataset includes all vendors who received one or more payments of $30,000 or more from Howard County, via the payment methods of Purchase Orders or Parked Documents, during the course of a fiscal year. Includes data from FY10 through FY24 and will be updated annually.

name: Tax Sales: 2010 - Current
description: The County holds an annual Tax Sale in May or June of each year. Tax lien certificates for properties with delinquent taxes, water and sewer bills or other liens will be auctioned to the highest bidder. On or about March 1, the County will send a delinquent notice to all property owners with delinquent bills and advertise the delinquent properties in the Howard County Times / Columbia Flier for at least four weeks prior to the sale.

description: One record for each incident on which Howard County Fire was called to assist another county.

name: Stormwater Management Inspections: January 2015 - Present
description: This is a month-by-month list of all stormwater management inspections performed or overseen by the Stormwater Management Division in the Bureau of Environmental Services. The list includes: Inspection Year and Month, which is the year and month in which inspection field work, additional investigation, and reporting was completed. Inspection Cycle and Sequence Number, which are scheduling and identification codes used by the Stormwater Management Division. Facility Type, which is the design of t

name: Fire And Rescue Formal Classroom Training Courses: 2012 - 2018
description: All sessions of training courses conducted at the Public Safety Training Center by HCDFRS or affiliate agencies. This data does not include frequent shift-based and station-based trainings of field personnel.

name: Age-Adjusted Death Rates for Selected Leading Causes: 2005 - 2023
description: This data set includes age-adjusted death rates as published by the Vital Statistics Administration, Maryland Department of Health for both Maryland and Howard County. The rates are adjusted to the 2000 U.S. standard population by the direct method and are calculated per 100,000 population. Single year age-adjusted rates are available for the five (5) leading causes of death in Howard County for 2020 - Heart Diseases, Malignant Neoplasms (Cancer), COVID-19, Cerebrovascular Diseases, and Accident

name: Office on Aging & Independence Caregiver Report
description: The National Family Caregiver Support Program is one of the federal Older Americans Act funded initiatives. This report reflects results collected on a quarterly basis and includes key demographics on individuals served with program dollars plus general overall numbers of caregivers served by various methods during the quarter. Methods of service include support groups, telephone, e-mail, education sessions, outreach sessions, newsletters and newspaper.

name: Howard County Tax Roll Report for Tax Year 2016

columns.name: STORM WATER CREDIT
columns.description: Credit against Watershed Protesction Fee for properties owned by non-profits who enter into an agreement with the County to mitigate stormwater runoff on the property.

columns.name: SHARED EQUITY CRED
columns.description: County tax credit on property jointly owned by the Howard County Housing Commission and a residential property owner.

columns.name: RELIGIOUS GROUP CRED
columns.description: County tax credit on property leased by a religious organization if the organization would have qualified for a property tax exemption under § 7-204 of the Tax Property Article of the Annotated Code of Maryland if it owned the property.

columns.name: PILOT
columns.description: County tax credit based on agreement between county and property owner to reduce taxes and take a Payment in Lieu of Taxes based on some other criteria.

columns.name: HIGH PRFM BLDG CRED
columns.description: County tax credit for LEED Certified Commercial and Residential Properties at a Silver level or higher.

columns.name: EMERGENCY WORKER
columns.description: County tax credit for Spouse of Fallen Law Enforcement Officer or Emergency Worker OR Disabled Law Enforcement Officer or Emergency Worker.

columns.name: CONSERV LANDS CREDIT
columns.description: County tax credits to owners of conservation land or property owned by a Land Trust.

columns.name: COMMUNITY ASSN CRED
columns.description: Tax credit on certain real and tangible personal owned by a Community Association.

columns.name: AGRICULTURE CREDIT
columns.description: Landowners with state and county agricultural easements may be eligible for this County tax credit.

columns.name: COUNTY HOMEOWNERS
columns.description: Supplemental Homeowner Credit funded by County.

columns.name: COUNTY HOMESTEAD
columns.description: County credit on County Tax which limits the increase in taxable assessment to 5% per year.

columns.name: STATE HOMEOWNER CRED
columns.description: State funded credit to assist homeowners with income below $60,0000.

columns.name: TAX TOTAL
columns.description: Total Taxes charged.

columns.name: WATERSHED PROTECTION
columns.description: Annual fee used to meet federal stromwater management requirements.

columns.name: STATE PROPERTY TAX
columns.description: Levied against properties to fund State services.

columns.name: FIRE TAX-METRO
columns.description: Levied against properties to fund Fire and Rescue Services in the County.

columns.name: AD VALOREM
columns.description: Levied on property located within the County's Metro water service district.

columns.name: COUNTY ASSESSMENT
columns.description: Assessment provided by the State on which County Tax, Fire Tax and Ad Valorem are charged.

columns.name: SENIOR TAX CREDIT
columns.description: A County Tax credit is available for homeowners who are at least 70 years old and have a combined household income that does not exceed 500% of the Federal poverty guidelines for a household of 2. For Tax year 2016, that limit is $81,200.

columns.name: FIRE HOMESTEAD CREDIT
columns.description: County credit on Fire Tax which limits the increase in taxable assessment to 5% per year.

columns.name: ENERGY DEVICE CREDIT
columns.description: Discontinued Tax Credit for residential installation of Energy Devices.

columns.name: BROWNFIELD
columns.description: County tax credit to the owner of a property who participates in a voluntary cleanup program with the State Department of the Environment.

columns.name: FRONT FOOT WATER
columns.description: Paid by owners of property located on a street in which a sewer main was built before 2004, for a period of 30 years.

columns.name: STATE HOMESTEAD
columns.description: State credit on State Tax which limits the increase in taxable assessment to 10% per year.

columns.name: HISTORICAL CREDIT
columns.description: County tax credit for certain improvements to qualified historic buildings.

columns.name: DEFERRED TAXES
columns.description: County tax deferral for elderly and disabled homeowners.

columns.name: COMM AND IND CRED
columns.description: County tax credits to commercial or industrial businesses that make a substantial investment in the County. This program is administered by the Economic Development Authority of Howard County.

columns.name: STATE BAY RESTOR FEE
columns.description: State charge billed on the Real Property bill for properties which are on well and septic.

columns.name: TRASH FEE
columns.description: Annual fee for the collection and disposal of residential trash, recycling and yard waste.

columns.name: COUNTY TAX
columns.description: Levied against properties to fund the General Services of the County.

columns.name: HISTORICAL RESTORATION
columns.description: County tax credit on the taxes related to the increase in the assessment of a property due to improvements on qualified historic buildings.

columns.name: DAYCARE CREDIT
columns.description: County tax credit on improvements made to property used exclusively for the purpose of providing family or group adult day care or child day care by a licensed or accredited provider.

columns.name: WATERSHED CREDIT
columns.description: Credit on Watershed Protection Fee for properties that take certain measures to reduce stormwater runoff.

columns.name: FRONT FOOT SEWER
columns.description: Paid by owners of property located on a street in which a water main was built before 2004, for a period of 30 years.

columns.name: TRASH CREDIT
columns.description: Homeowners with limited income may qualify for a 60% credit against the Trash Fee.

columns.name: AMATEUR SPORTS CRED
columns.description: County tax credit on property that includes athletic fields that are used exclusively for amateur sports and the owner is a non-profit organization.

columns.name: THERAP RIDING CREDIT
columns.description: County tax credit on property for therapeutic riding facilities that furnish services to disabled individuals, at least 85% of its clients are disabled and the owner is a non-profit organization.

columns.name: ROUTE 1
columns.description: County tax credit for properties on Route 1 for the cost of exterior improvements.

columns.name: HISTORICAL SPRINKLER
columns.description: County tax credit for the installation of sprinkler systems in qualified historic buildings.

columns.name: LIVABLE HOMES
columns.description: County tax credit for the cost of certain accessibility features installed in owner occupied residential property.

columns.name: WATERSHED HARDSHIP
columns.description: Credit on Watershed Protection Fee for low income homeowners.

The data shows ridership in Howard County, Anne Arundel County, Prince Georges County, the City of Laurel, and Baltimore County. The data also includes other transit services in Howard County, including HoCo RapidRide (on-demand microtransit) and the Old Ellicott City Trolley (circulator bus).
